title: Coelopleurum gmelinii
taxonomy: Coelopleurum gmelinii is a species of plant in the family Apiaceae. It is native to Russia and Eastern Asia. This plant is a perennial herb and belongs to the genus Coelopleurum. The classification of Coelopleurum gmelinii is further divided into its kingdom, which is Plantae, and its family ranking, which is also Apiaceae. It is a part of the larger class of Magnoliopsida.
uses: Coelopleurum gmelinii, also known as the Gmelin's angelica, is a plant native to the mountainous regions of East Asia.  Traditional medicine in this region utilizes the plant for its purported health benefits.  Some sources suggest that the plant may have anti-inflammatory properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.  It is also used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and fever.  Further studies are required to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications.

intro: Coelopleurum gmelinii, also known as Gmelin's angelica, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and diuretic effects, making it beneficial for alleviating symptoms of arthritis, menstrual cramps, and urinary issues. The bioactive constituents of Coelopleurum gmelinii include volatile oils, flavonoids, and lignans, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
